string.description:
Identifier of the theme to activate. Send the reserved sentinel
`"default"` to clear the active theme and fall back to the built-in
default.

list.description: Use 'scopes' instead. The attributions selected define the scope to monitor.

string.description: Type of comparison for the alert threshold (used with `operator` and `value`). If omitted on create, defaults to `percentage-change`.
Currency code for monetary values.
Data source used to query data for the alert. Affects which dimensions and metrics are available.

Add a dimension to break down the evaluation of the condition. For example, evaluate a condition over an attribution for each "Service". Must be a dimension key returned by GET /analytics/v1/dimensions. Not allowed with condition: `forecast`. Used when you Investigate an alert, the dimension becomes the report grouping.
Identifier for metric type (e.g., basic, custom, extended).

single_nested.description: Define how metrics are selected and filtered in reports.

string.description: Text/operator used to filter metric values in metric filters (gt = greater than, lt = less than).

bool.description: If true, string matching is case-insensitive. Effective only for starts_with, ends_with, and contains modes; ignored otherwise.

string.description: Dimension key to filter on. Must pair with `type` and match a dimension returned by `GET /analytics/v1/dimensions` (for example, `service_description` with `type: fixed`). For `allocation_rule`, use `allocation_rule`. For `allocation`, use the allocation group ID. See `DimensionsTypes` for how each `type` uses `id`.

bool.description: Include rows where the dimension is null. If includeNull is omitted, behavior defaults to `false`.
Set to `true` to exclude the set values. If inverse is omitted, behavior defaults to `false`.

string.description: Controls how the dimension’s `values` are matched when the alert query runs. If mode is omitted, behavior defaults to is.

Dimension filter type. Always pair `type` with `id` on scope filters. Discover valid `id` + `type` pairs for your account with `GET /analytics/v1/dimensions`. `allocation_rule` replaces `attribution`; `allocation` replaces `attribution_group`.

list.description: List of values to include or exclude. Must match exact strings from your billing or DataHub data for the dimension (for example, `Amazon Simple Storage Service` for AWS S3 on `service_description`). For `allocation_rule`, use allocation rule IDs.

list_nested.description: The filters selected define the scope of the alert. Each item is a Cloud Analytics filter (same idea as report filters). Only costs/usages matching all scope logic are included in the alert.

string.description: The period each evaluation looks at.

float64.description: The `condition` threshold value. For example, actual metric threshold value for the `value` condition, or percentage change threshold value for the `percentage-change` condition.

single_nested.description: Parameters that define when and how an alert is evaluated.

string.description: Name of the alert (max 64 characters).

list.description: List of emails to notify when the alert is triggered.  If omitted on create, defaults to the API user’s email. Must match allowed customer domains.

int64.description: The time when the alert was created (in UNIX timestamp).

string.description: Alert ID.

int64.description: Last time the alert was triggered (in UNIX timestamp).
Last time the alert was modified (in UNIX timestamp).

schema.description: Notifications triggered when cloud costs exceed defined thresholds or meet specific conditions.
schema.markdown_description: Notifications triggered when cloud costs exceed defined thresholds or meet specific conditions.

string.description: Allocation description.
Identifier of the folder that contains the allocation. Set to "root" if the allocation is at the top level (not in a folder).
Allocation name.

bool.description: If true, string matching is case-insensitive. Effective only for starts_with, ends_with, and contains modes; rejected otherwise.
Include null values.
If true, all selected values will be excluded.
If true, all selected values will be excluded.

string.description:
Key of an existing dimension. Examples: "billing_account_id", "country". When type is "allocation_rule", the key must be set to "allocation_rule".
Use `GET /analytics/v1/dimensions` to retrieve all available dimensions.
Filter mode to apply. When type is "allocation_rule", only "is" and "contains" modes are supported.
Dimension filter type for allocation rule components. See `DimensionsTypes` for per-value meanings. Allocation components do not support `allocation`, `attribution`, or `attribution_group` types.

list.description: Values to filter on. When type is "allocation_rule", the values are IDs of existing allocation rules.

list_nested.description: List of allocation filter components.

string.description: Formula for combining components (A is the first component, B is the second one, etc.).

single_nested.description: Single allocation rule. Components can reference other existing allocation rules by using the "allocation_rule" dimension type.

string.description: Action to perform with this rule.

bool.description: If true, string matching is case-insensitive. Effective only for starts_with, ends_with, and contains modes; rejected otherwise.
Include null values.
If true, all selected values will be excluded.
If true, all selected values will be excluded.

string.description:
Key of an existing dimension. Examples: "billing_account_id", "country". When type is "allocation_rule", the key must be set to "allocation_rule".
Use `GET /analytics/v1/dimensions` to retrieve all available dimensions.
Filter mode to apply. When type is "allocation_rule", only "is" and "contains" modes are supported.
Dimension filter type for allocation rule components. See `DimensionsTypes` for per-value meanings. Allocation components do not support `allocation`, `attribution`, or `attribution_group` types.

list.description: Values to filter on. When type is "allocation_rule", the values are IDs of existing allocation rules.

list_nested.description: List of allocation filter components (required for 'create' or 'update' action). Can include components of type "allocation_rule" to reference existing allocation rules.

string.description: Description of the allocation rule.
Formula for combining components (A is the first component, B is the second one, etc.)
ID of existing allocation (required for 'update' or 'select' action).
Name of the allocation rule.
Custom label for values that do not fit into allocation (required for group type allocation).
Composition type of allocation.

bool.description: Whether anomaly detection is enabled for this allocation.

int64.description: The time when the allocation was created (in UNIX timestamp).

string.description: Allocation ID.
Type of allocation (preset or custom).

int64.description: Last time the allocation was modified (in UNIX timestamp).

string.description: The unique identifier of the subscription.

single_nested.description: The start and end dates of an annual commitment term.

bool.description: Whether this subscription is on an annual commitment plan (true) or a flexible plan (false).

string.description: The name of the plan, e.g. ANNUAL_MONTHLY_PAY, ANNUAL_YEARLY_PAY, FLEXIBLE, or TRIAL.

single_nested.description: Plan details for a subscription, indicating whether it is a commitment or flexible plan.

string.description: The purchase order ID associated with this subscription, if any.
The type of renewal, e.g. AUTO_RENEW_MONTHLY_PAY, AUTO_RENEW_YEARLY_PAY, RENEW_CURRENT_USERS_MONTHLY_PAY, RENEW_CURRENT_USERS_YEARLY_PAY, SWITCH_TO_PAY_AS_YOU_GO, or CANCEL.

The number of committed seats. Applies to annual commitment plans.

string.description: The unique SKU identifier for the subscribed product.
A human-readable name for the subscribed product SKU.
The current status of the subscription, e.g. ACTIVE, SUSPENDED, or PENDING.

single_nested.description: Subscription details for a G Suite or Office 365 asset.
Additional properties associated with an asset, such as subscription and customer details.

string.description: The asset type, e.g. g-suite, office-365, google-cloud, amazon-web-services, or microsoft-azure.

int64.description: The time when the asset was created, in milliseconds since the epoch.

string.description: A human-readable label for the asset, typically combining the product name and domain or account ID.
A link to the asset details page in the DoiT console.

schema.description: Manage cloud provider connections and check feature availability for connected accounts.
schema.markdown_description: Manage cloud provider connections and check feature availability for connected accounts.

string.description: Cloud provider that a connection is bound to.

Provider-specific configuration object. Required fields depend on `cloudProvider`.
See CloudFlow connection documentation for the expected shape per provider.
. Value is JSON-encoded.
Human-readable description.

Client-provided connection ID. Server assigns one if omitted. Providing a
stable ID enables idempotent creation.
Unique display name for the connection within the tenant.
ISO 8601 (UTC) creation timestamp. `null` on dry-run create.

bool.description:
Whether this connection is enabled. Derived from `status`: `false` when
`status` is `disabled`, `true` otherwise. Toggle via
`PATCH /connections/{connectionId}` with `{ "enabled": true/false }`.

string.description: Strong ETag. `null` on dry-run create.
ISO 8601 (UTC) timestamp of the last successful sync with the cloud provider.

Operational status of a connection.
- `active` — the connection is valid and in use.
- `disabled` — disabled via `PATCH /connections/{connectionId}`.
- `pending_permissions` — awaiting a permission grant in the cloud provider.
- `permissions_failed` — permission setup failed.
- `inactive` — deactivated.
- `cloud_deleted` — the underlying cloud resource was deleted externally.
- `draft` — connection is not yet fully configured.
Human-readable explanation of a non-active status.
ISO 8601 (UTC) last-modified timestamp.
Unique identifier of the connection.
Human-readable description.

Operator instructions or notes attached to the flow. Pass `null` or
omit to leave empty.
Display name for the new flow.
ISO 8601 timestamp (UTC) when the flow was created. `null` on dry-run create.

Strong ETag for this resource version. Pass as `If-Match` on subsequent mutations.
`null` on dry-run create responses.
ID of the trigger (first) node. `null` if no trigger node has been set.

Server-assigned unique identifier. `null` on dry-run create responses
(no resource was persisted).
ISO 8601 timestamp (UTC) of the most recent execution. `null` if never executed.
Status string of the most recent execution. `null` if never executed.

ISO 8601 (UTC) timestamp of the next scheduled execution. `null` if the flow
has no scheduled trigger, is unpublished, or scheduling is paused.
Identifier of the specific operation this node performs (e.g. AWS service action).
Identifier of the integration or application this node belongs to.

Approval request message body. May contain inline text, upstream node

node's output field resolved at runtime by the flow engine).

int64.description:

Connection provider type for `connection` variables.
Required when `type` is `connection`.
Human-readable description.
Element type for `list` variables. Required when `type` is `list`.

Variable name. Alphanumeric, underscore, and dash only. Must be unique
within scope (flow-local or global).

bool.description: Whether the flow requires a value before publishing.

string.description:
Data type of a variable.
- `string` — UTF-8 text.
- `integer` — 64-bit signed integer.
- `float` — 64-bit floating-point number.
- `boolean` — true / false.
- `list` — ordered collection; element type specified by `elementType`.
- `connection` — reference to a connection resource; provider type specified by `connectionType`.

Initial or updated value, always string-encoded on the wire. Encoding by `type`:
- `string` → the string value
- `integer` → decimal string, e.g. `"42"`
- `float` → decimal string, e.g. `"3.14"`
- `boolean` → `"true"` or `"false"`
- `list` → JSON-encoded array string, e.g. `"[\"a\",\"b\"]"`
- `connection` → connection ID string; the referenced connection must exist
  within the tenant and be in an `active` or `inactive` state.
ISO 8601 (UTC) creation timestamp. `null` on dry-run create.

string.description: Data source of the report.
The identifier of the dimension.

Dimension filter type. Always pair `type` with `id` on scope filters. Discover valid `id` + `type` pairs for your account with `GET /analytics/v1/dimensions`. `allocation_rule` replaces `attribution`; `allocation` replaces `attribution_group`.

list_nested.description: See [Dimensions](https://help.doit.com/docs/cloud-analytics/reports/editing-your-cloud-report#dimensions).

string.description: Font size used for axis labels on charts.
Font size used for data labels on charts.

int64.description: Number of decimal places shown for numeric values.

string.description: Scale applied to numeric values when rendering the report.

Identifier of the theme applied to the report. The reserved
sentinel `"default"` is returned on GET when no theme is stored
and clears the stored value on PATCH. Omit the field on PATCH
to leave the stored value unchanged.

single_nested.description: Display settings for the report.

string.description: See [View data as (Comparative report)](https://help.doit.com/docs/cloud-analytics/reports/editing-your-cloud-report#view-as).

bool.description: If true, string matching is case-insensitive. Effective only for starts_with, ends_with, and contains modes; ignored otherwise.

string.description: Dimension key to filter on. Must pair with `type` and match a dimension returned by `GET /analytics/v1/dimensions` (for example, `service_description` with `type: fixed`). For `allocation_rule`, use `allocation_rule`. For `allocation`, use the allocation group ID. See `DimensionsTypes` for how each `type` uses `id`.

bool.description: Include rows where the dimension is null. If includeNull is omitted, behavior defaults to `false`.
Set to `true` to exclude the set values. If inverse is omitted, behavior defaults to `false`.

string.description: Controls how the dimension’s `values` are matched when the alert query runs. If mode is omitted, behavior defaults to is.

Dimension filter type. Always pair `type` with `id` on scope filters. Discover valid `id` + `type` pairs for your account with `GET /analytics/v1/dimensions`. `allocation_rule` replaces `attribution`; `allocation` replaces `attribution_group`.

list.description: List of values to include or exclude. Must match exact strings from your billing or DataHub data for the dimension (for example, `Amazon Simple Storage Service` for AWS S3 on `service_description`). For `allocation_rule`, use allocation rule IDs.

list_nested.description: The filters to apply to the report.

For basic metrics, the value can be one of: ["cost", "usage", "savings"]
If using custom metrics, the value must refer to an existing custom metric ID.

int64.description: The number of items to show

string.description: Dimension filter type. Always pair `type` with `id` on scope filters. Discover valid `id` + `type` pairs for your account with `GET /analytics/v1/dimensions`. `allocation_rule` replaces `attribution`; `allocation` replaces `attribution_group`.

list_nested.description: The rows that appear in the tabular format of the report. See [Group by](https://help.doit.com/docs/cloud-analytics/reports/editing-your-cloud-report#group-by).

bool.description:
Whether to include [promotional credits](https://help.doit.com/docs/cloud-analytics/reports/editing-your-cloud-report#promotional-credits).
If set to **true**, the report must use time interval `month`, `quarter`, or `year`.

bool.description:
Whether to align to complete previous periods (full year/quarter/month) vs shifting dates by amount.

    When `true`, selects complete periods (e.g., full previous year Jan 1-Dec 31, not up to today).
    When `false`, shifts dates by amount, which may result in partial periods extending to today.

string.description: Time interval unit for shifting.

If split type is "attribution_group", then target type must be "attribution".
Otherwise split types and target types must be the same.

float64.description: Percent of the target, represented in float format. E.g. 30% is 0.3. Required only if the Split Mode is custom.

Time unit for the time range.

single_nested.description:
Specify a predefined or custom time range for the report.
For example, to specify a custom time range of "last 2 days", set the mode to `last`, the amount to `2`, and the unit to `day`. If `includeCurrent` is `true`, the range will be yesterday and today; otherwise, the range will be yesterday and the day before yesterday.
If "custom" type is specified, you need to provide a custom time range in the `customTimeRange` field.
Report configuration.

string.description: Report description.
Identifier of the folder that contains the report. Set to "root" if the report is at the top level (not in a folder).

Report ID.
Report type.
